core_mechanism: percolation-style connectivity breakdown of a network under node removal, where survival of a giant connected component depends on the heterogeneity of the degree distribution and on whether nodes are removed at random versus in order of decreasing degree (hub-targeted)
brief_reason: The abstract contrasts robustness of connectivity under random node failure with rapid loss of connectivity when the highest-connectivity nodes are selectively removed, which is a percolation/giant-component threshold governed by the network's degree distribution.
